description:
You are acting as a technical‐writer who uses a tiny SQLite catalogue to track what documentation files already exist.  
A database file is already stored at  

    /home/user/doc_management/doc_index.db  

Inside that database there is exactly one table, named **documents**, whose schema is  

    documents(  
        doc_id     INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,  
        title      TEXT    NOT NULL,  
        category   TEXT    NOT NULL  
    );  

and it currently contains three rows.

Your job is to export the full contents of the *documents* table to a **CSV file** using a *single* terminal command.

1. Create the file  

       /home/user/doc_management/documents_export.csv  

2. The command must:
   • run a SQL query that selects **all** columns from *documents*  
   • sort the rows by **doc_id** ascending  
   • output the result in standard CSV format, **including** a header row  

3. The resulting /home/user/doc_management/documents_export.csv file **must match this exact layout** (no extra spaces, tabs or blank lines):  

    doc_id,title,category  
    1,Getting_Started,Guide  
    2,API_Reference,Reference  
    3,Release_Notes,Changelog  

An automated test will:
• confirm the file exists at the precise path shown,  
• verify its content is byte-for-byte identical to the block above.

Only one shell command should be used to perform the export; no intermediate files are allowed.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts
-----------------------------------------
1. Directory: /home/user/doc_management  (already created, mode 755)
2. File: /home/user/doc_management/doc_index.db
   • SQLite 3 database
   • Contains table:
        CREATE TABLE documents(
            doc_id   INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
            title    TEXT NOT NULL,
            category TEXT NOT NULL
        );
   • Rows inserted:
        (1, 'Getting_Started', 'Guide')
        (2, 'API_Reference',   'Reference')
        (3, 'Release_Notes',   'Changelog')

Expected artefact after successful task
---------------------------------------
File: /home/user/doc_management/documents_export.csv
Exact ASCII content (10 LF-terminated lines, no CR characters):

doc_id,title,category\n
1,Getting_Started,Guide\n
2,API_Reference,Reference\n
3,Release_Notes,Changelog\n

(Where “\n” represents the single newline character 0x0A.)

The test suite will open /home/user/doc_management/documents_export.csv and compare it verbatim to that content.
